author: mark
text:
SPEC: clone layout — drop hashing, nest paths transparently

Fourth revision of SPEC-clone-layout. Drops the SHA-256-truncated <origin-key> / <path-hash> identity in favor of percent-encoded URL/branch components + literal nested clone paths. Every on-disk path is now hand-readable; the §10 hand-operable sequence no longer calls sha256sum.

Introduces clone.json (per-on-disk-checkout, uncommitted under ~/.config/balls/<nested-clone-path>/), completing the three-layer shadowing rule for layered fields: clone.json ?? repo.json ?? project.json ?? built-in default. Tracker-scope fields (version, id_length, min_bl_version, plugins) are owned by project.json and rejected outside it.

Stealth/no-git mode is absorbed into clone.json (stealth: true + tasks_dir), retiring the synthetic-origin-key carve-out. The active/ and own/ view-symlink trees are retired. §11 grows a new bl-fc50 → bl-e9c2 migration column. §14 conformance tests revise the hashing-related cases to test path-based identity. No code changes in this ball.
